implementation_difficulty_reason: Requires new Delta S = 1 charged-LFV semileptonic Wilson coefficients and matching from a lepton-bulk or leptoquark RS extension, but the physical observable is a clean two-body upper limit with standard kaon decay-constant and quark-mass inputs and no long-distance SM subtraction.
priority_rationale: Deferred by DA-4 (round_004_addendum_deferred_scope.md); LFV-kaon trio deferred as a lepton-bulk RS scope extension; quark-only RS does not naturally generate s->d e mu. Promoted in Wave-8 because: Top-tier per SESSION_NOTES.md sec 7; one of three explicit lepton-bulk-RS handles in the kaon sector. Lower implementation priority than Waves 1-7 PRIMARY entries.

evidence: No exact K019 / K_L -> e mu / s->d e mu implementation found in quarkConstraints, qcd, flavorConstraints, neutrinos, yukawa, warpConfig, solvers, scanParams, or tests.
Adjacent lepton LFV code is mu->e gamma only: flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1 documents a mu->e gamma constraint, and lines 15-21 tie the rate to the (Ybar_N Ybar_N^dagger)12 dipole spurion.
The scanner consumes only the mu->e gamma LFV check: scanParams/scan.py:523-535 calls check_mu_to_e_gamma and records the reason string mu_to_e_gamma.
Adjacent kaon code is quark Delta F=2: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:610-624 defines kaon hadronic parameters for epsilon_K and Delta m_K, not semileptonic LFV K_L decay.
grep_commands: rg -n "K019|K_L|KL|K0_L|K\\^0_L|kaon.*LFV|LFV.*kaon|lepton.?flavo[u]?r.*kaon" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "e\\s*mu|mu\\s*e|electron.*muon|muon.*electron" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "Delta.?S.?=.?(1|one)|s.?->.?d|sd.*(electron|muon|lepton)|d.*s.*e.*mu|s.*d.*e.*mu|Wilson.*lepton|semileptonic.*kaon" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
rg -n "mu.?->.?e|muToE|mu_to_e|mu.*e.*conversion|charged.?lepton|LFV|lepton.?flavo[u]?r" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "K_L\\s*->\\s*(e|mu)|KL\\s*->\\s*(e|mu)|K0_L\\s*->\\s*(e|mu)|K\\^0_L\\s*->\\s*(e|mu)|e\\^\\{?\\\\pm\\}?\\s*mu|mu\\^\\{?\\\\pm\\}?\\s*e|muon.*electron.*kaon|electron.*muon.*kaon" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

perez_randall_lepton_bulk_context.source_type: paper-era lepton-bulk RS reference; not a pdg_or_equivalent measured observable
perez_randall_lepton_bulk_context.relevance: Lepton-bulk RS / lepton-MFV context for charged-lepton flavor violation and why K019 is a scope extension beyond quark-only RS.

charged_lfv_review.source: PDG 2025 Tests of Conservation Laws review
charged_lfv_review.relevance: Qualitative CLFV context: charged-lepton flavor mixing has not been observed; neutrino-mass-only effects are unobservably small, so accessible charged LFV implies BSM flavor sources.

post_2010_developments.why_matters: Includes K_L -> mu e and K_L -> pi0 mu e in a broader charged-LFV pattern study, useful for relating kaon LFV to charged-lepton and B-meson LFV observables.

Updates RS charged-lepton LFV calculations in a full 5D framework; does not compute K019 directly but fixes the lepton-sector misalignment ingredients needed for any lepton-bulk RS K019 implementation.
Studies warped custodial models with partially composite leptons, rare K decays, and lepton-sector flavor violation; relevant to embedding K019 in non-universal lepton RS scenarios.
Explicit RS-adjacent leptoquark example noting that K_L -> mu e and K -> pi mu e stringently constrain conventional Pati-Salam vector-leptoquark scales.
Post-2008 strange-hadron rare-decay prospects report; included as context for renewed rare-strange experimental activity, not as a direct update to the K_L -> e mu limit.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(K_L \to e^{\pm}\mu^{\mp}\).  This entry covers
the charged-lepton-flavor-violating two-body decay of the long-lived neutral
kaon, summed over the two charge assignments.  It is a null-search channel:
the Standard Model with only neutrino mixing gives no observable charged-LFV
kaon rate, so any signal would point to new flavor structure.
sections.pdg_prose:
The canonical sidecar value is the PDG 2025 \(K_L^0\) listing limit
\[
  {\cal B}(K_L\to e^{\pm}\mu^{\mp}) < 4.7\times 10^{-12}
  \quad (90\%~{\rm CL}).
\]
The value is listed by PDG as \(\Gamma(e^{\pm}\mu^{\mp})/\Gamma_{\rm total}\)
and is the BNL E871 final result of Ambrose et al.; see
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K019/pdg2025\_kl\_emu.txt} and
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/K019/ambrose\_1998\_arxiv\_hepex9811038.txt}.
sections.relevance:
This is a \textbf{SECONDARY} Wave-8 entry; see
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/PRIORITY\_TIERS.md} for the tier policy.  The process
was deferred because the quark-only RS flavor pipeline does not naturally
produce an \(s\to d e\mu\) amplitude.  It becomes relevant once bulk leptons,
lepton-flavor-nonuniversal gauge couplings, Pati--Salam leptoquark gauge
bosons, or other lepton-sector flavor spurions are included.

In such extensions, \(K_L\to e\mu\) constrains products of \(s\)-\(d\) and
\(e\)-\(\mu\) flavor-violating couplings.  Neutral KK gauge exchange or a
flavor-changing \(Z\) can generate vector/axial semileptonic operators, while
scalar or Higgs-mediated structures probe chirality-enhanced pseudoscalar
matrix elements proportional to the kaon decay constant and quark masses.
Dipole effects are less direct for this two-body mode but become part of the
same lepton-bulk RS flavor story when the charged-lepton misalignment is not
minimal-flavor-violating.
sections.post_2008:
No newer direct \(K_L\to e\mu\) branching-fraction limit appears in the
PDG 2025 \(K_L^0\) listing; the BNL E871 result remains the canonical bound.
Post-2008 work matters mainly through model interpretation.  Buras et al.
(\texttt{arXiv:1006.5356}) included \(K_L\to\mu e\) and
\(K_L\to\pi^0\mu e\) in a broad charged-LFV pattern study, providing a useful
template for comparing kaon LFV with charged-lepton and \(B\)-meson LFV
channels.  Beneke, Moch, and Rohrwild (\texttt{arXiv:1508.01705}) updated the
RS charged-lepton LFV calculation in a full 5D framework, emphasizing dipoles,
\(\mu\to 3e\), and muon conversion rather than kaon modes, but fixing the
lepton-bulk flavor ingredients needed before K019 can be predicted.

D'Ambrosio and Iyer (\texttt{arXiv:1712.08122}) studied warped custodial
models with partially composite leptons, rare \(K\) decays, and lepton-sector
flavor violation.  Blanke and Crivellin (\texttt{arXiv:1801.07256}) are the
most explicit RS-adjacent post-2008 handle for this entry: their
Pati--Salam-in-RS setup notes that conventional vector-leptoquark scenarios
are stringently constrained by \(K_L\to\mu e\) and \(K\to\pi\mu e\).  The LHCb
strange-hadron prospects report (\texttt{arXiv:1808.03477}) is included only
as experimental/prospective context, not as a replacement for the \(K_L\)
limit.
sections.validity:
The experimental limit is robust and clean as a bound on the physical
branching fraction.  Its use as an RS constraint is model-dependent.  A
minimal quark-only RS implementation should not impose this as a primary
constraint, because the \(e\)-\(\mu\) flavor transition is absent.  A
lepton-bulk or leptoquark-extended implementation can use it as a strong
constraint after specifying the charged-lepton flavor basis, the relevant
semileptonic Wilson coefficients, and whether vector, axial, scalar,
pseudoscalar, or Higgs-mediated operators dominate.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Exact K019 searches over \texttt{quarkConstraints/},
\texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/},
\texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no
\(K_L\to e^{\pm}\mu^{\mp}\), \(s\to d e\mu\), or kaon semileptonic LFV
implementation.  The nearby code surfaces are different observables:
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1} and
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:15--21} document a
\(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole constraint,
\texttt{scanParams/scan.py:523--535} consumes only that LFV check, and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:610--624} defines neutral-kaon
\(\Delta F=2\) inputs rather than a charged-LFV kaon decay.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{MEDIUM.}  The observable itself is experimentally clean, but the repo
would need a new \(\Delta S=1\) charged-LFV semileptonic operator interface,
matching from the chosen lepton-bulk or leptoquark RS extension, and a kaon
decay-rate evaluator with vector/axial and scalar/pseudoscalar matrix
elements.  No new long-distance SM subtraction is the main simplifying
feature, but the necessary operator basis is not currently present.

\texttt{LHCbStrange2018\_Prospects}.

priority_rationale_short: Very tight LFV kaon cap for lepton-extended RS models
priority_rationale_full:
K_L -> e mu has an extremely clean forbidden-SM signature and a very tight
branching-fraction upper limit. It can strongly constrain products of
s-d and e-mu flavor-violating couplings in lepton-bulk, leptoquark, or
nonuniversal gauge extensions of RS. It is not HIGH for the current catalog
because the quark-only anarchic scan does not naturally generate the required
charged-lepton flavor transition.
